# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Istio RequestAuthentication policy for JWT validation. Configure jwks and issuer settings to validate incoming tokens. Set up rules to extract JWT claims from Authorization headers. This is for our API authentication layer.
# Category: istio_jwt_validation_weak

apiVersion: security/v1alpha1
kind: RequestAuthenticationPolicy
metadata:
  name: example-policy
spec: {
   "selector": {"matchLabels" :{"app":"example"} }, # Label selector for the pod(service) that will use this policy. Optional, if not specified it can be used with all services in namespace where Istio is installed or globally by setting 'global' to true (optional). 
   "targetSelector": {"matchLabels" :{"app":"example"} }, # Target selector for the ServiceAccount which contains JWKS and will use this policy. Required if targetServiceAccountRef isn’t specified here, it can be used with all services in namespace where Istio is installed or globally by setting 'global' to true (optional).
   "jwtRules": [{ # A list of rules for JWTs that will use this policy.  Required if jwks and issuerRef are not specified here, it can be used with all services in namespace where Istio is installed or globally by setting 'global' to true (optional).
     {   "claimMatch": {"name":"iss", "matchType" : "'exact'"},  # The Issuer claim name. Required if issuerRef isn’t specified here, it can be used with all services in namespace where Istio is installed or globally by setting 'global' to true (optional).
         "claimMatch": {"name":"sub", "matchType" : "'exact'"},  # The Subject claim name. Required if issuerRef isn’t specified here, it can be used with all services in namespace where Istio is installed or globally by setting 'global' to true (optional).
         ...   },...]    }]}`}
